description_long: This is a yellow, single-propeller airplane with a high-wing configuration and a classic, robust fuselage. The wings are straight and mounted above the main body, with visible struts connecting them to the lower fuselage for added stability. The airplane features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both accented with red trim. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a smaller tail wheel, all fixed and exposed, suitable for rugged or unpaved surfaces. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with clear windows, and appears to be designed for one or two occupants. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the overall design suggests a utility or bush plane suitable for short takeoff and landing operations.
description: A yellow, high-wing, single-propeller airplane with a conventional tail, fixed landing gear, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the yellow fuselage, high-mounted straight wing, fixed landing gear, and red-accented tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, enclosed cockpit, wing struts, and the symmetrical high-wing layout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same high-wing, fixed gear, and tail configuration, with clear visibility of the cockpit windows.
description_view_3: Head-on view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit glazing, wing struts, and the wide stance of the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with external struts and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with clear windows, likely single or dual seat.
